message:
🌱 dragonfly: Dragonfly does not work with nest/bull  (#1281)

* 🌱 Add dragonfly: Dragonfly does not work with nest/bull  mission

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: correct dragonfly namespace to dragonfly-system

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "dragonfly-782-dragonfly-does-not-work-with-nest-bull",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "dragonfly: Dragonfly does not work with nest/bull ",
+    "description": "Dragonfly does not work with nest/bull . This issue affects 5+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify dragonfly troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your dragonfly deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n dragonfly-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly -n dragonfly-system --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review dragonfly configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant dragonfly configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n dragonfly-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly\nkubectl get configmap -n dragonfly-system -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dragonfly\n```\nSee this https://github.com/BlackYuzia/Dragonfly-Bull to reproduce.\n\n1. Dragonfly rejects EVAL under MULTI (which could potentially be fixed by making the multi TX global - dynamically)\n2."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Dragonfly does not work with nest/bull ",
+        "description": "Removes `OpRem` and `OpSet` assertions that check the new element is not empty. Running locally both work fine with empty elements.\n\nEmpty elements in `LREM` and `LSET` are supported by Redis, eg `EVAL \"redis.call('RPUSH', 'mylist', 'one'); redis.call('LSET', 'mylist', 0, '');\" 0`, and are used by bullmq (https://github.com/taskforcesh/bullmq/blob/master/src/commands/includes/cleanList.lua#L36) (see https://github.com/dragonflydb/dragonfly/issues/782)\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Dragonfly does not work with nest/bull  is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly -n dragonfly-system --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n dragonfly-system --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Removes `OpRem` and `OpSet` assertions that check the new element is not empty. Running locally both work fine with empty elements.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
